creator: niall@eaglepiservices.com
text:
I can boot into FreeBSD using the .iso available here: 

ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/releases/i386/ISO-IMAGES/7.0/

I chose the boot only ISO. 

This ISO boots just fine when I don't have my SATA card installed, but halts on boot when I do install it. 

My PCI SATA Card is: 

Part Number: G61020000000016 (PROMISE TECHNOLOGY INC)

The website for the manufacturer and the product is here: 

http://www.promise.com/product/product_detail_eng.asp?product_id=191

Full Specifications: Promise Technology Promise FastTrak TX4650
Full Specs Type	Storage controller (RAID)
Interface Type	Serial ATA-300 / SAS
Required Slot Type	PCI Express x1
Form Factor	Plug-in card
Supported Devices	Hard drive, Disk array (RAID)
Manufacturer Part No.	FTTX4650
UPC	704118023011
Dimension	2.7 in x - x 4.4 in (HxWxD)

This card is quite inexpensive and I'm sure is quite popular. 

The data prior to the BTX Halted message is: 

--------------------------------------------------------------

int=0000000d    err=00000000     efl=00030246     eip=000004fc
eax=00000000    ebx=00000000     ecx=00002000     edx=00000080
esi=00000000    edi=3fec0000     ebp=00009079     esp=000003e0
cs=d000   ds=9800   es=0000    fs=9079   gs=0000   ss=9079
cs:eip= 67 66 26 89 07 83 c6 04-66 83 c7 04 e2 ef b8 20
        00 e8 75 02 66 58 75 05-66 8b f8 eb 09 e8 06 02
ss:eip= 00 00 ec 3f ae 48 00 00-00 00 0c 00 00 00 00 00 
        00 00 00 00 00 00 9a 05-08 0c 00 08 00 00 00 00

         
--------------------------------------------------------------

Fix: 

I have no idea. I'm sorry. 

I'm just trying to report something I think is amiss.
How-To-Repeat: Install one of those cards in your machine.
